title: Late-round findings on unchanged code
body:
## Context

From PR #223 review experience: round 5 raised findings on code unchanged since round 1.

## Problem

Each review round re-examines the entire diff, potentially raising new speculative findings on code that was already reviewed. This creates an infinite loop where:

1. Round N reviews code, raises findings
2. We fix findings, push
3. Round N+1 reviews same code again, finds new angles
4. Repeat

## Examples

- HTTP hardening finding in round 5 on client code from round 1
- Truncation "off-by-one" findings on code we added in round 1

## Potential Solutions

1. Track which code regions have been reviewed and approved
2. Only review changed lines in subsequent rounds
3. Stop after convergence (0-1 new findings = done)
4. Weight findings lower if they're on previously-reviewed code

## Priority

Medium - causes review fatigue in multi-round PRs.
